ops.title: ship fetch-first + auto-rebase landed (450e524b); conflicted-bookmark guard; codex-wrapper background-wait trap; cc-context AGENTS/CLAUDE are cc-guides rendered artifacts

ops.body:
`ccx vcs ship` fetch-first + auto-rebase landed on main 2026-07-16 as 450e524b6897, released as ccx v0.21.0 (brew), and the cc-skills entry-13 + version-control fragment updates shipped at cc-skills 647ffd32/7427f102 (task b29295028b closed). The conflict-rollback path was proven live during the fleet migration (write-like-me): follow ship's printed recipe, but note `cc-guides render` cannot parse a conflict-markered lock — restore the lock from your side (`git show <commit>:.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock >`) before rendering, then `jj squash` + `jj bookmark move exact:main --to @-` + push. See [[guides-fleet-remigration]]. shipPushJJ: `jj git fetch` first (hard error), divergence = trunk-lane nearest-bookmark 0-match or --bookmark ancestry probe empty, then do–check–undo rebase in `jjRebaseOnto` (stack `bookmarks(exact:X)..@-`, op-id capture, `jj rebase -b @- --destination` — use `--destination`, a permanent alias; `--onto` hard-fails on older jj — conflict check `conflicts() & (bookmarks(exact:X)..@-)::` over the WHOLE rewritten set incl. @ and sibling descendants, `jj op restore` on any conflict). A missing/conflicted (multi-head) target bookmark is refused pre-mutation — a conflicted bookmark otherwise becomes a silent merge destination for `jj rebase --destination`. Resume after rebase-ok/push-fail takes the no-divergence path (verified live). Untracked trunk (`jj git init --colocate` default!) still fails loudly at push — auto-track decision is backlog task 706d5d1c. `exact:`+name quoting deliberately unchanged (note 455c715e: Go %q would regress unicode names; needs a jj-string escaper).

**Codex-wrapper background-wait trap**: a codex-wrapper inside a Workflow "paused" awaiting a background-task notification and the workflow captured that pause message as its final result while codex kept running detached — the work completed but its report was lost. Workflow/subagent prompts to wrappers must demand synchronous waits; on a premature return, check `ps`/`jj st` for the detached process's actual output before re-dispatching.

**cc-context AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are cc-guides RENDERED artifacts** (marker in line 1): local fragments live in `.claude/fragments/AGENTS.md/` (`go-style.fragment.md` holds Version control; the ccx entries table is the shared `cc-skills:ccx` fragment pinned in `cc-guides.lock`). Hand-edits get clobbered by the next render — edit the fragment, run `cc-guides render`, and commit fragment + lock + every regenerated artifact together (a PreToolUse hook enforces this). Related: [[cc-guides-fragment-pipeline]], [[ship-scoped-commits-bookmark-guard]], [[ship-ci-watch-overhaul]].
